# growthbook_fact_metric (Resource)

A GrowthBook fact metric built on top of a fact table. Supports proportion, mean, ratio, quantile, retention, and dailyParticipation metric types.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A proportion metric: fraction of users with at least one order.
resource "growthbook_fact_metric" "purchase_rate" {
  name        = "Purchase rate"
  metric_type = "proportion"
  datasource  = "ds_warehouse"
  projects    = [growthbook_project.web.id]

  numerator = {
    fact_table_id = growthbook_fact_table.orders.id
    column        = "$$distinctUsers"
  }
}

# A ratio metric: average order amount per purchasing user.
resource "growthbook_fact_metric" "avg_order_value" {
  name        = "Average order value"
  metric_type = "ratio"
  datasource  = "ds_warehouse"

  numerator = {
    fact_table_id = growthbook_fact_table.orders.id
    column        = "amount"
    aggregation   = "sum"
  }

  denominator = {
    fact_table_id = growthbook_fact_table.orders.id
    column        = "$$distinctUsers"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `metric_type` (String) Metric type: `proportion`, `retention`, `mean`, `quantile`, `ratio`, or `dailyParticipation`.
- `name` (String) Human-readable fact metric name.
- `numerator` (Attributes) Reference to a fact table column, including aggregation and filters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--numerator))

### Optional

- `archived` (Boolean) Whether the fact metric is archived.
- `capping_settings` (Attributes) Controls how outliers are handled. Write-only: not refreshed from the server. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--capping_settings))
- `denominator` (Attributes) Reference to a fact table column, including aggregation and filters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--denominator))
- `description` (String) Description of the fact metric.
- `display_as_percentage` (Boolean) Display ratio/dailyParticipation means as a percentage.
- `inverse` (Boolean) Set true when a decrease is good (e.g. bounce rate).
- `managed_by` (String) Where the metric is managed from: empty, `api`, or `admin`.
- `max_percent_change` (Number) Maximum percent change considered significant (proportion, e.g. 0.5 for 50%).
- `metric_auto_slices` (List of String) Slice column names automatically included in metric analysis (enterprise).
- `min_percent_change` (Number) Minimum percent change considered significant (proportion, e.g. 0.005 for 0.5%).
- `min_sample_size` (Number) Minimum sample size before showing results.
- `owner` (String) Owner userId or email address.
- `prior_settings` (Attributes) Controls the bayesian prior for the metric. Write-only: not refreshed from the server. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--prior_settings))
- `projects` (List of String) Associated project IDs.
- `quantile_settings` (Attributes) Settings for quantile metrics (required when metric_type is `quantile`). Write-only: not refreshed from the server. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--quantile_settings))
- `regression_adjustment_settings` (Attributes) Controls regression adjustment (CUPED) settings. Write-only: not refreshed from the server. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--regression_adjustment_settings))
- `tags` (List of String) Tags associated with the fact metric.
- `target_mde` (Number) Target minimum detectable effect (proportion, e.g. 0.1 for 10%).
- `window_settings` (Attributes) Controls the conversion window for the metric. Write-only: not refreshed from the server. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--window_settings))

### Read-Only

- `datasource` (String) Data source ID (derived from the numerator's fact table).
- `date_created` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC3339).
- `date_updated` (String) Last update timestamp (RFC3339).
- `id` (String) Unique fact metric identifier.

<a id="nestedatt--numerator"></a>
### Nested Schema for `numerator`

Required:

- `fact_table_id` (String) Fact table ID this metric column references.

Optional:

- `aggregate_filter` (String) Comparison applied after aggregation, e.g. `>= 1` (numerator only). Requires aggregate_filter_column.
- `aggregate_filter_column` (String) Column used to filter users after aggregation (numerator only; retention/proportion metrics).
- `aggregation` (String) User aggregation of the column: `sum`, `max`, `count distinct`, `hll merge`, or `kll merge`.
- `column` (String) Column name, or a special value such as `$$distinctUsers` or `$$count`. Must be empty for proportion metrics.
- `filters` (List of String) Fact table filter IDs applied to this column.


<a id="nestedatt--capping_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `capping_settings`

Optional:

- `ignore_zeros` (Boolean) Ignore zeros when computing a percentile cap.
- `type` (String) Capping type: `none`, `absolute`, or `percentile`.
- `value` (Number) Absolute value or percentile (0.0-1.0) depending on type.


<a id="nestedatt--denominator"></a>
### Nested Schema for `denominator`

Required:

- `fact_table_id` (String) Fact table ID this metric column references.

Optional:

- `aggregation` (String) User aggregation of the column: `sum`, `max`, `count distinct`, `hll merge`, or `kll merge`.
- `column` (String) Column name, or a special value such as `$$distinctUsers` or `$$count`. Must be empty for proportion metrics.
- `filters` (List of String) Fact table filter IDs applied to this column.


<a id="nestedatt--prior_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `prior_settings`

Optional:

- `mean` (Number) Prior mean of relative effects, in proportion terms.
- `override` (Boolean) If false, organization default prior settings are used.
- `proper` (Boolean) If true, use the configured mean and stddev; otherwise use an improper flat prior.
- `stddev` (Number) Prior standard deviation of relative effects (> 0).


<a id="nestedatt--quantile_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `quantile_settings`

Optional:

- `ignore_zeros` (Boolean) Ignore zero values when calculating the quantile.
- `quantile` (Number) Quantile value (0.001 to 0.999).
- `quantile_event_count_column` (String) Override for the per-row event count column (event quantile metrics with a `kll merge` numerator).
- `type` (String) Quantile over `event` values or `unit` aggregations.


<a id="nestedatt--regression_adjustment_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `regression_adjustment_settings`

Optional:

- `days` (Number) Number of pre-exposure days used for the adjustment.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether regression adjustment is applied.
- `override` (Boolean) If false, organization default settings are used.


<a id="nestedatt--window_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `window_settings`

Optional:

- `delay_unit` (String) Delay unit: `minutes`, `hours`, `days`, or `weeks`.
- `delay_value` (Number) Delay after exposure before counting conversions.
- `type` (String) Window type: `none`, `conversion`, or `lookback`.
- `window_unit` (String) Window unit: `minutes`, `hours`, `days`, or `weeks`.
- `window_value` (Number) Length of the conversion window.
